---
title: 'Inerrancy... !?'
date: '2023-03-09T15:59:00.0050000+00:00'
draft: false
categories:
  - 'Musings'
---

Inerrancy...&nbsp;<div><br /></div><div><div><a href="https://www.premierunbelievable.com/ask-nt-wright-anything/ask-nt-wright-anything-154-tom-answers-qs-on-universalism-hell-and-salvation/14803.article" target="_blank">Ask NT Wright Anything #154 Tom answers Qs on universalism, hell and salvation</a></div><div><br /></div>
<br />
Inerrancy is a dead end road, that has no purpose or benefit. That is a summary of Tom's (NT Wright) response to the topic in this podcast. It was a concept born illegitatemly from the enlightment rationalism movements of the 1700-1800s.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>That got me thinking, is the American Church good for anything? Like, the longer I deconstruct modern western American Christianity, look into the Bible for what it is and not what we tried to make it, and connect with the living-breathing-vibrant history of the Bible and various worldwide Christian mvoements for 2,000 +/- years...&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>The more I wonder if we're the ones who need to be "saved", from ourselves.</div><div>
